back_translation: And Joab son of Zeruiah knew that the heart of the king was upon Absalom.

And Joab sent to Tekoa and took from there a wise woman, and said to her, 'Please mourn, and please put on garments of mourning, and do not anoint yourself with oil, and be like a woman who for many days has been mourning over the dead.
And go to the king and speak to him according to this word.' And Joab put the words in her mouth.
And the woman of Tekoa spoke to the king, and fell on her face to the ground and bowed down and said, 'Save, O king!'
And the king said to her, 'What is to you?' And she said, 'Truly a widow woman am I, and my husband is dead.
And your maidservant had two sons, and the two of them quarreled in the field, with no one to deliver between them; and the one struck the other and killed him.

And behold, the whole clan has arisen against your maidservant and said, Hand over the one who struck his brother, that we may put him to death for the life of his brother whom he killed; and we will destroy also the heir. And they would extinguish my ember that remains, to leave to my husband neither name nor remnant upon the face of the earth.'
And the king said to the woman, 'Go to your house, and I will give orders concerning you.'
And the woman of Tekoa said to the king, 'Upon me, my lord the king, be the iniquity, and upon the house of my father; and the king and his throne be guiltless.'
And the king said, 'Whoever speaks to you, bring him to me, and he shall not touch you again.'

And she said, 'May the king please remember the LORD your God, that the avenger of blood may not increase the destruction, and they shall not destroy my son.' And he said, 'As the LORD lives, not a hair of your son shall fall to the ground.'
And the woman said, 'Let your maidservant please speak to my lord the king a word.' And he said, 'Speak.'

And the woman said, 'Why then have you devised such a thing against the people of God? And in the king's speaking this word, it is as one guilty, in that the king does not bring back his banished one.

For dying we shall die, and like waters that are poured out on the ground, which cannot be gathered; and God does not take away a soul, but devises plans so that the banished one may not be banished from Him.

And now, that I have come to speak to the king my lord this word — because the people made me afraid — and your maidservant said, I will speak please to the king; perhaps the king will do the word of his maidservant.
For the king will hear, to deliver his maidservant from the hand of the man, to destroy me and my son together from the inheritance of God.
And your maidservant said, May the word of my lord the king be for rest; for as an angel of God, so is my lord the king to hear the good and the evil; and may the LORD your God be with you.'
And the king answered and said to the woman, 'Do not please conceal from me a word that I ask you.' And the woman said, 'Let my lord the king please speak.'

And the king said, 'Is the hand of Joab with you in all this?' And the woman answered and said, 'As your soul lives, my lord the king, none can go to the right or to the left from all that my lord the king has spoken; for your servant Joab — he commanded me, and he put in the mouth of your maidservant all these words.
For the sake of changing the face of the matter your servant Joab has done this thing; but my lord is wise, like the wisdom of an angel of God, to know all that is in the land.'
And the king said to Joab, 'Behold please, I have done this thing; and go, bring back the young man Absalom.'

And Joab fell on his face to the ground and bowed down and blessed the king; and Joab said, 'Today your servant knows that I have found favor in your eyes, my lord the king, that the king has done the word of his servant.'
And Joab arose and went to Geshur and brought Absalom to Jerusalem.
And the king said, 'Let him turn to his house, and my face he shall not see.' So Absalom turned to his house, and the face of the king he did not see.
And like Absalom there was no man so handsome in all Israel as to be praised greatly; from the sole of his foot and to the top of his head there was no blemish in him.

And in his shaving his head — and it was from the end of days to days that he would shave it, because it was heavy upon him and he would shave it — he weighed the hair of his head two hundred shekels by the king's stone.
And to Absalom were born three sons and one daughter, and her name was Tamar; she was a woman beautiful of appearance.
And Absalom dwelt in Jerusalem two years of days, and the face of the king he did not see.
And Absalom sent for Joab to send him to the king, but he was not willing to come to him; and he sent again a second time, but he was not willing to come.
And he said to his servants, 'See, the field of Joab is by my hand, and he has there barley; go and set it on fire.' And the servants of Absalom set the field on fire.
And Joab arose and came to Absalom at his house, and said to him, 'Why have your servants set the field that is mine on fire?'

And Absalom said to Joab, 'Behold, I sent to you, saying, Come here, and I will send you to the king, saying, Why have I come from Geshur? Better for me still I were there. And now, let me see the face of the king; and if there is iniquity in me, then let him put me to death.'
And Joab came to the king and told him; and he called to Absalom, and he came to the king, and bowed to him on his face to the ground before the king; and the king kissed Absalom.
